items.description: Sample size: Uses an approximately 20,000-instance working dataset and documents the sample size clearly
Source and target: Identifies the data source and uses Cover_Type classes 1 through 7 consistently
Enterprise Overleaf: Includes a working read-only Overleaf link associated with the student's Georgia Tech / Enterprise Overleaf account
Enterprise GitHub: Includes a valid commit hash from the student's GT Enterprise GitHub repository, accessible to course staff
Run instructions: Provides clear commands, environment requirements, data paths, and seeds sufficient to reproduce outputs
Sampling procedure: Provides the sampling method, sample size, random seed, and enough detail to recreate the sampled dataset
Readable figures and tables: Figures, tables, axis labels, legends, captions, and text are readable at normal grading zoom
Outside sources: Includes at least two relevant peer-reviewed sources beyond course materials and uses them to justify choices or interpret results
AI usage statement: Includes a clear AI/LLM usage statement identifying tools, how they were used, and affirming student review/understanding

items: Report does not use an approximately 20,000-instance Covertype working dataset or does not document sample size clearly
Data source is unclear, target mapping is ambiguous, or Cover Type classes are not treated as classes 1 through 7
Overleaf link is missing, broken, requires edit permission, or appears to use a personal Overleaf account
GitHub commit hash is missing, invalid, inaccessible, or hosted only in a personal GitHub repository
Run instructions are missing or insufficient to reproduce results
Sampling procedure is vague or the sampled dataset cannot be recreated
Multiple figures or tables are too small, cropped, blurry, poorly labeled, or unreadable
Peer-reviewed outside sources are missing, irrelevant, only listed without use, or not connected to methodology/interpretation
AI/LLM usage statement is missing, vague, incomplete, or does not affirm student review and understanding

items.description: Full-data EDA: Includes full-dataset shape, feature types, missingness or absence of missingness, class counts, and class proportions
Sample EDA: Provides class counts and class proportions for the approximately 20,000-instance sample
Sampling rationale: Stratification is used and justified, or non-stratified sampling is defended with evidence that all classes are preserved
Ordering effects: Uses a randomized or stratified procedure, or provides a compelling reason for a different choice
Metric justification: Explains why Accuracy, Macro-F1, balanced accuracy, confusion matrix, and per-class precision/recall/F1 are appropriate and why accuracy alone is insufficient
Early hypothesis: At least one hypothesis is stated after EDA and before tuned results, tied to dataset properties and course theory
Hypothesis revisit: Evaluates the hypothesis using metrics, learning curves, model-complexity curves, runtime, or error patterns
Test holdout: Test set is held out and used only once for final reporting
Training-only validation: Hyperparameter tuning uses CV or fixed validation on training data only; preprocessing fit only on training data/folds

items: Full-data EDA is absent, superficial, incorrect, or not connected to the task
Sample class counts/proportions are missing or unclear
Sampling is described as random without evidence, rationale, or comparison to the full class distribution
Student appears to use head(20000) or the first rows without justification
Metrics are merely listed, only accuracy is meaningfully discussed, or why accuracy alone is insufficient is not explained
Hypothesis is just a goal, not testable, not tied to dataset/course theory, or stated after results
Hypothesis is not revisited or is ignored in the discussion
Hyperparameters appear to be chosen based on test performance or final test set is repeatedly used for model selection
Validation is unclear, appears to include test data, or preprocessing appears to be fit before splitting

items.description: Complexity control: Identifies a control such as max_depth, min_samples_leaf, or ccp_alpha and explains how it limits overfitting
Learning curve: Shows training and validation metric versus at least four training sizes and uses it to discuss bias/variance
Complexity curve: Varies at least three reasonable values of a pruning/regularization parameter and supports final model choice
Final evaluation: Reports Accuracy, Macro-F1, balanced accuracy, and includes confusion matrix or per-class precision/recall/F1
Runtime: Reports fit and predict time with at least one trade-off
Tuning quality: Tests at least three reasonable hyperparameter values and justifies the final choice using validation or model-complexity evidence

items: No pruning/regularization mechanism is used or explained
Learning curve is missing, has fewer than four training sizes without justification, is mislabeled, or is not interpreted
Complexity curve is missing, uses too few values, varies an unrelated parameter, or is not interpreted
Required metrics are missing or error patterns are not discussed
Runtime is missing or only one timing value is reported without context
Final model appears arbitrary, only one setting is tested, or tested values are too narrow to support the conclusion

groups.label: D1. Scaling, Distance and k Exploration

items.description: Scaling and distance: Scaling is leakage-safe, and the distance metric and weighting choices are stated
k exploration: At least three distinct k values are tested and include meaningfully different neighborhood sizes
Learning curve: Shows training and validation metric versus at least four training sizes and is interpreted
Complexity curve: Shows validation metric versus at least three distinct k values and supports the final model choice
Final evaluation: Reports Accuracy, Macro-F1, balanced accuracy, and includes confusion matrix or per-class precision/recall/F1
Runtime: Reports fit and predict time, and prediction-time cost is discussed
Sensitivity discussion: Discusses sensitivity to irrelevant features, high dimensionality, mixed continuous/binary features, or class imbalance

items: Scaling-sensitive preprocessing is missing, unclear, or fit before splitting
Only one k is tested, fewer than three values are tested without justification, or tested values are too narrow
Learning curve is missing, has fewer than four training sizes, is mislabeled, or is not interpreted
Complexity curve is missing, uses too few k values, or is not interpreted
Required metrics are missing or error patterns are not discussed
Runtime is missing or prediction-time cost is ignored
No kNN-specific limitation is discussed

items.description: Kernel comparison: Results from at least two kernels, such as linear and RBF, are reported and compared
Scaling: Scaling is leakage-safe and applied consistently
Learning curve: Shows training and validation metric versus at least four training sizes and is interpreted
Complexity curve: Varies C and/or gamma across at least three reasonable settings total, or provides a justified smaller grid for computational reasons
Final evaluation: Reports Accuracy, Macro-F1, balanced accuracy, and includes confusion matrix or per-class precision/recall/F1
Runtime: Reports fit and predict time and kernel trade-offs are discussed
Tuning quality: At least two kernels are evaluated, C is varied for each kernel, and gamma is varied for RBF/nonlinear kernels

items: Only one kernel is used or kernels are mentioned but not actually compared
Scaling is missing, unclear, or fit before splitting
Learning curve is missing, has fewer than four training sizes, is mislabeled, or is not interpreted
Complexity curve is missing, uses too few values without justification, varies irrelevant parameter, or is not interpreted
Required metrics are missing or error patterns are not discussed
Runtime is missing or kernel runtime trade-offs are ignored
Final model appears arbitrary, kernel choice is unsupported, or C/gamma tuning is missing without justification

items.description: SGD setup: Optimizer is explicitly SGD, no disallowed optimizers are used, and learning rate, batch size, epochs, early stopping if used, and regularization are documented for both implementations

groups.label: F2. Epoch, Learning and Complexity Curves

items.description: Epoch curve: Shows train and validation loss or metric over epochs for both scikit-learn and PyTorch implementations and interprets optimization behavior
Learning curve: Shows training and validation metric versus at least four training sizes and is interpreted
Complexity curve: Varies at least three reasonable neural network settings, such as width, depth, learning rate, or regularization, and supports final model choice
Final evaluation: Reports Accuracy, Macro-F1, balanced accuracy, and includes confusion matrix or per-class precision/recall/F1 for NN models
Implementation comparison: Compares scikit-learn and PyTorch in optimization behavior, stability, runtime, tunability, or implementation differences under SGD-only constraints
Runtime and tuning: At least three reasonable NN settings are tested, and fit/predict time or epoch-level training cost is reported and interpreted

items: Momentum, Nesterov, Adam, AdamW, Adagrad, RMSprop, or another adaptive optimizer is used; or training setup is not documented
Epoch evidence is missing, shown for only one implementation without justification, or not interpreted
Learning curve is missing, has fewer than four training sizes, is mislabeled, or is not interpreted
Complexity curve is missing, uses too few settings, varies irrelevant setting, or is not interpreted
Required metrics are missing or error patterns are not discussed
Both implementations are reported but not meaningfully compared
Final model appears arbitrary, too few settings are tested without justification, or runtime/training cost is missing

items.description: Cross-model synthesis: Directly compares at least three required algorithm families and explains behavioral differences using evidence from metrics, curves, runtime, and/or error patterns
Performance and efficiency trade-offs: Explains trade-offs between predictive performance and computational cost with at least two concrete comparisons
Class-level behavior: Analyzes multiclass performance, identifies easiest/hardest cover types, discusses minority-class behavior, and explains misclassification patterns
Curve-based interpretation: Uses learning curves and model-complexity curves to make at least two specific claims about underfitting, overfitting, variance, bias, or hyperparameter sensitivity
Limitations and improvements: Identifies at least one concrete limitation and at least one evidence-based improvement or follow-up experiment

items: Models are presented separately without comparison, only best model is stated, or claims are unsupported
Runtime and metrics are reported separately but trade-offs are not explained, computational cost is ignored
Class imbalance is ignored, only overall accuracy is reported, minority classes are not discussed, or confusion matrix is not interpreted
Curves are included but only described visually or not used to support claims about bias, variance, or tuning behavior
Limitations are missing, generic, or disconnected from results; improvements are generic and not evidence-based

sections.main_section: Extra Credit: Activation Study (up to 5 points)

items.description: Activation curves: Epoch-wise validation curves are shown for all four activations using the same training budget
Comparison table: A final comparison table reports metrics, runtime, and convergence notes
Interpretation: Explains convergence stability and trade-offs under SGD

items: No extra credit implemented
Extra credit uses a disallowed optimizer; no extra credit should be awarded
Activation curves are missing, not comparable, or use different training protocols
Comparison table is missing or incomplete
Activation study only reports numbers without interpretation
